title: Wrong result for partition table with order by + limit
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
create table t(a int, b int, index idx_bc(a, b)) PARTITION BY HASH (`b`) PARTITIONS 4;
insert into t values (1,2),(1,3),(1,1),(1,5);
analyze table t;
select * from t where a = 1 order by b limit 10;
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

```
mysql> select * from tcommonhash where a = 1 order by b limit 10;
+------+------+
| a    | b    |
+------+------+
|    1 |    1 |
|    1 |    2 |
|    1 |    3 |
|    1 |    5 |
+------+------+
4 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
mysql> select * from tcommonhash where a = 1 order by b limit 10;
+------+------+
| a    | b    |
+------+------+
|    1 |    1 |
|    1 |    3 |
|    1 |    5 |
|    1 |    2 |
+------+------+
4 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->

Only on master branch
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(a INT, b INT, c VARCHAR(10), PRIMARY KEY (a, b, c)) PARTITION BY HASH (b) PARTITIONS 4;
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE a = 1 ORDER BY b LIMIT 10;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT a, b FROM t1 WHERE b IS NOT NULL) AS subquery ORDER BY b DESC LIMIT 5;
key_bug_features: partition table with order by and limit
